note: Verified user-reachable coverage gaps. Each confirmed real by corpus search. Burn down RED-first in Phase 3.

backlog.title: Enable push notifications (request permission)
backlog.recommendation: Add a RED-first Playwright spec at apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/push-opt-in.spec.ts (or extend notification-inbox.spec.ts) that signs up/onboards/unlocks PIN, navigates to a /_workspace child route, deterministically enables push (test seam for VITE_ONESIGNAL_APP_ID and a page.addInitScript stub of Notification.requestPermission so no real OS prompt fires), then asserts getByTestId('push-opt-in') is visible, clicks getByTestId('push-opt-in-enable'), and asserts the nudge disappears AND localStorage[PUSH_OPT_IN_DISMISSED_KEY]==='1' AND the stubbed permission request was invoked.

backlog.title: Portal root redirects to Appointments tab
backlog.recommendation: Add one RED-first Playwright e2e that, as an authenticated patient session (Better-Auth user with linked Person+dental_patient and NO membership), runs page.goto(`${APP}/portal`) and asserts the redirect: await page.waitForURL(/\/portal\/appointments$/) plus expect(page.getByTestId('portal-tab-appointments')).toHaveAttribute('aria-current','page'). Seed the patient session via the J24 magic-link/Mailpit helper. Prove RED by temporarily removing the `throw redirect` in portal.index.tsx (URL stays at /portal), then restore.

backlog.title: Bottom tab navigation between Appointments and Bills
backlog.recommendation: Add ONE journey/e2e spec (e.g. apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/journeys/32-patient-portal-tabs.journey.spec.ts, or a simpler portal-tabs.spec.ts) that, as a PATIENT session (auth-only, no PIN — reuse the magic-link/session setup from 24-patient-magic-link-signin.journey.spec.ts so it lands authenticated): 1) navigate to /portal (or /portal/appointments) and assert the bottom nav is visible with both tabs; 2) start on Appointments — assert MyAppointmentsView content is shown and `getByTestId('portal-tab-appointments')` has aria-current="page"; 3) click `getByTestId('portal-tab-bills')`, assert URL becomes /portal/bills, MyInvoicesView (/me/invoices + /me/balance roll-up) renders, and aria-current flips to the Bills tab; 4) click back to `portal-tab-appointments` and assert it returns. Write it RED first (the route/view/testids already exist, so it should go GREEN once the patient session is correctly established — make the patient-session setup the deliberate failing precondition, e.g. assert you reach /portal not /auth/pin). This drives the tab control end-to-end and exercises the /me/* endpoints through the real UI.

backlog.title: Coverage authorization (LOA) lifecycle backing claims (inter-module)
backlog.recommendation: RED-first, smallest viable: a UI-driving Playwright e2e is currently impossible because the LOA UI does not exist (no route, no "Add authorization" control, orphaned hooks). So add an API-level lifecycle e2e/journey that walks the LOA flow through the live stack, mirroring billing journeys 25-27: (1) create an insurance profile for a seeded patient, (2) POST /dental/patients/{patientId}/authorizations with insuranceProfileId, (3) GET /dental/patients/{patientId}/authorizations and assert the new LOA appears with status 'pending', (4) PATCH /dental/patients/{patientId}/authorizations/{authorizationId}/status to 'approved' with approvedAmountCents and assert the transition + persisted amount. Place it at apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/loa-authorization-lifecycle.spec.ts (or a numbered journey). This is the honest minimal lock. The full fix is a follow-up: surface an "Add authorization" control (wire the existing usePatientAuthorizations/useAuthorizationMutations hooks into InsuranceCard or a patient-profile panel) and then add a true UI-driving Playwright journey.

backlog.title: Portal reads invoices/balance via dental-billing facade (boundary lint)
backlog.recommendation: Add a RED-first Playwright e2e at apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/portal-bills.spec.ts that authenticates as a patient and navigates to /portal/bills, asserting the facade end-to-end: balance roll-up via getByTestId('portal-balance-amount') from GET /me/balance, own invoices via getByTestId('portal-invoices-list')/portal-invoice-card from GET /me/invoices (with a voided/uncollectible invoice confirmed hidden in the UI), and an IDOR sanity check that only the caller's data appears.

backlog.title: Route a clicked push to its in-app deep link
backlog.control: OS/browser push notification click (OneSignal click listener)
backlog.recommendation: Add one Playwright e2e spec at apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/push-deep-link.spec.ts that, on the authenticated /_dashboard shell, page.addInitScript a window.OneSignal stub (Notifications.addEventListener captures the click handler; force the SDK's isInitialized truthy so the real usePushNotificationRouting listener is registered). Then invoke the captured handler with additionalData {type:'booking.created'} and assert navigation to /calendar; add cases type:'recall.due'->/patients, type:'billing'->/billing, unknown type->/dashboard (never a dead tap). This exercises the full _dashboard -> hook -> listener -> resolveNotificationDeepLink -> navigate chain the pure unit test cannot. Write it RED-first.

backlog.title: Inter-module: findBillingParty — invoice merchant/customer existence validation
Patient views own outstanding balance roll-up (GET /me/balance)
backlog.recommendation: Add one RED-first Playwright journey spec (e.g. apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/journeys/32-portal-view-my-balance.journey.spec.ts) modeled on J24. Set B / skipAllowed (needs an authenticated patient session). Steps: establish a patient session for an account whose person owns a dental_patient row with at least one issued invoice carrying a balance; page.goto(`${APP}/portal/bills`); assert expect(page.getByTestId('portal-balance-summary')).toBeVisible(); then do an independent read page.request.get(`${API}/me/balance`) via the browser's own cookie jar and assert the rendered outstanding amount equals body.outstandingBalanceCents. RED today (no spec drives /portal/bills), GREEN once the live control is proven to reflect the endpoint.

backlog.title: Patient views own appointments (GET /me/appointments)
backlog.recommendation: Add a Playwright e2e journey, e.g. apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/portal-appointments.spec.ts, RED-first against current main. Sign in as a seeded patient who owns at least one appointment, navigate to ${APP}/portal/appointments, and assert DOM-first (Anti-Cheating Rule 1): [data-testid="portal-appointments-list"] visible with at least one [data-testid="portal-appointment-card"] whose visible text matches the seeded visit type/when. Add an independent read (Rule 2) via page.request.get(`${API}/me/appointments`) on the browser cookie jar, asserting the same appointment id appears. For the flagged "Try again" control, also force GET /me/appointments to fail (route.fulfill 500), reload, assert the role="alert" copy + visible "Try again" button, then click it and assert it re-fires GET /me/appointments. Standard authed Postgres lane (no Mailpit/MinIO); seed patient+appointment via API as J31/online-booking specs do.

backlog.title: Chat room WebSocket (real-time chat + WebRTC signaling)
backlog.recommendation: Add a server-level WebSocket integration test (not a Playwright UI journey, since comms has no UI in apps/dentalemon). Boot api-ts, connect a real WebSocket client to /ws/comms/chat-rooms/:room. RED-first smallest test: assert an unauthenticated/invalid-token connection is rejected (handshake closed), exercising the live middleware+onConnect chain the config-shape unit test cannot. Then extend to authenticated connect + message broadcast to a second client + onClose cleanup.

backlog.title: Mark all notifications as read (optionally by type)
backlog.recommendation: Add an e2e test to notification-inbox.spec.ts that seeds 2+ real notifications, opens the bell, clicks notification-mark-all, and asserts the empty state + badge removal via the live POST /notifs/read-all round-trip.

backlog.title: Sign out of patient portal
backlog.recommendation: Add a focused portal sign-out journey (e.g. apps/dentalemon/tests/e2e/journeys/32-portal-sign-out.journey.spec.ts), Set B / skipAllowed since establishing the patient session most cleanly reuses Mailpit. RED-first smallest path: (1) get a patient session (reuse J24's magic-link helper, or seed a patient and sign in via API into the browser cookie jar); (2) page.goto(`${APP}/portal/appointments`) and assert getByTestId('portal-sign-out') is visible (proves /portal/* loaded under the auth-only guard); (3) drive the control while capturing the call: await Promise.all([page.waitForResponse(r => /\/auth\/sign-out/.test(r.url()) && r.request().method()==='POST'), page.getByTestId('portal-sign-out').click()]) and assert status 200; (4) goal-state independent read (Rule 2): expect redirect to /auth/sign-in AND page.request.get(`${API}/auth/get-session`) now returns a null/empty user, proving the session was actually destroyed — not merely that the button exists.

backlog.title: List due recalls (front-desk recare worklist)
backlog.recommendation: Add a be-unit test (e.g. services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-patient/recalls/listDueRecalls.test.ts) mounting listDueRecalls at GET /dental/recalls/due via a buildTestApp-style harness, with an authenticated branch member. Smallest RED-first assertions: (1) 200 returns only in-window non-terminal (pending/sent) recalls enriched with patientName, excluding out-of-window and completed/cancelled — proving the handler's window+projection (not just the repo); (2) 403 for an authenticated non-member of branchId (assertBranchAccess); (3) 400 on malformed from/to (e.g. from=2026-13-99) — the YYYY-MM-DD ValidationError guard; (4) default window applied when from/to omitted (seed one due today vs one +60d, assert only today's appears). Do NOT duplicate 401-no-auth/route-existence — already covered by reminders-route-registration.test.ts.

backlog.title: Export structured chart snapshot
backlog.recommendation: Add a handler-level bun test (e.g. services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-visit/exportDentalChart.test.ts) mirroring the sibling dental-pmd.data-portability.test.ts harness (buildTestApp + seeded org/branch/membership/person/patient/visit). RED-first, assert the handler's untested branches that buildChartExport cannot cover: (1) 401 when no authenticated user; (2) 404 NotFoundError('Dental visit') for an unknown visitId; (3) branch-access denial via assertBranchAccess for a visit on a branch the user lacks; (4) 404 when the patient is missing; (5) baseline-fallback path — when the visit has no chart row, teeth come from DentalChartBaselineRepository.findByPatient; (6) cross-visit treatment aggregation — a treatment on a DIFFERENT visit of the same patient appears in the exported summary (proving the inArray(visitIds) join at lines 57-64). Start with the cheapest RED, the 404 on unknown visitId.

backlog.title: List consent refusals for visit
backlog.recommendation: Add a RED-first bun unit test for the listConsentRefusals handler.

backlog.title: BR-003 — no clinical writes to a locked/completed visit
backlog.recommendation: BR-003 locked/completed-visit guard exists in createPrescription.ts:37-40 but no test asserts it on the prescription-create path. Add a RED negative-path test: create prescription on a locked visit → expect 409/BusinessLogicError.
evidenceFiles: services/api-ts/src/handlers/dental-clinical/prescription.status.test.ts:310-329 (PATCH path only, not the named POST create endpoint)

contractOnlyDeferred.title: Apply patient credit against invoice (atomic draw-down)
Credit apply cap: > 0, <= balance, <= available credit (BR-052)
AR aging buckets per patient + summary
Refund cap: non-void payment/invoice, amount <= un-refunded remainder
Read branch fee schedule (CDT catalog with effective prices)
Set a per-branch CDT price override
Create blocked without a person identity (MISSING_PERSON_PROFILE)
One patient profile per person (PATIENT_EXISTS / unique index)
Self-registration grants patient role; staff creation does not
CIMG-003: landmark FSM strictly forward placed→confirmed→locked
Fee resolution precedence: branch override → catalog default → 0
Per-site gingival margin must be integer -5..20mm
BR-P04 FDI tooth number must be a valid quadrant range
BR-P06 tooth-reading upsert idempotent on (chartId, toothNumber)
Update single ceph landmark (transition + lock guard)
